OUEST ION
As the chief of police for the city of Barsto\"/, are you
required to disclose on your statement of economic interests your
advisory board positions with the Desert Community Bank and the
san Bernardino County centra]- credit Union?
CONCLUS ION
At this time there is no obligation for you to report any
business positions on the statement of econonic interests. 
This
advice may change once the City of Barstow corrects its conflict
of interest code (see analysis below) .
L/ covernnent Code Sections 81OOO-91015. A.Ll- statutory
references are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated.
Comrnissj,on regulations appear at 2 California Code of ReguJ,ations
Section 18000, et seo. A11 references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the californj.a Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8Oo o P.O. Box 8o? o Sacramento CA 958o4-O8o7 . (916) 322-5660


Page Two
May ‚Ė†7‚Ä≤  ‚Ė†99‚Ė†
FACTS
As the chief of police for the city of Barstor,r, you are
designated in the city's conflict of interest code to file 
a
statement of Econonic Interests Form 73o. The city's conflict of
interest code requires you to disclose on schedules A, D, F and c.
Schedule G is a business position schedule. You are a rnenber
of an advisory board to the Desert community Bank and to the San
Bernardino County Central Credit Union. You are asking if the
advisory board positions are required to be disclosed on your
Statement of Economic Interests.
ANALYS TS
The Political Reform Act requires every agency to adopt and
pronulgate a confLict of interest code. (Section 87300) A
conflict of interest code is a docunent which designates the
positions within the agency which nake or participate in the
naking of decisions which may foreseeably have a materj-al effect
on any financial interest and further sets forth the tvpes of
investrnents, business oositions, incorne and real propertv rnade
reportable bv those desiqnated emplovees. (Section 87302 t
ernphasis added. )
The responsibility for determining if a code meets the
specifications of the Act rests with the rrcode reviewing bodyl
who, in this instance, is the Barstow city council. (Section
87303.) Fo!.this reason the Commission can only provide inf orrnal
assistance. z/
The conflict of interest code for the City of Barstow does
not set forth the specific types of investnents, business
positions, income and real property which are reportable by the
positions designated in the code. The code sets out schedul-es
vrhich the city has determined should be completed but fails to set
forth the tvDes of investments, business positions, income or real
property to be reported on those schedules. The code for the City
of Barstow does not meet the requirements of Section 97302.
For example, a properly formulated code might require the
discLosure of investments and business positions in business
entities, 
and incorne from sources which rnanufacture or supply
eguiprnent, rnachinery, supplies. or services of the type ulLa Uy
2/ rnfornal assistance
irnmunity provided by an
(Government Code Section
1832e (c) (3) . )
does not provide the requestor with the
opin‚Ė†on or formal wr‚Ė†tten adv‚Ė†ce.
83‚Ė†‚Ė†4, 2 Cal. COde of Regs. section

May 17, 19 91
the city. 
A person assigned this
her investments in such an entitY category would disclose his or
on schedules A and C-2; business
positions on schedule G and income on scheduLes D, E, F and H-
The city attorney's office will receive a copy of this letter
and be apprised of this error in the code. 
once the code is
corrected, should it require the di-sclosure of investnents and
business positions in business entities, and income from sources
which are banks or lending institutions, 
we would have to
reexamine this issue to deterrnine if your advisory board position
constitutes a position of managernent and should be reportable.
Until that tirne, it does not appear that you need to disclose any
business positions.
I hope this inforrnation has been helpful to you. If I can be
of further assistance pfease feel free to caII me at (9]6) 322-
5901.
